description:
An aerospace engineering firm needs a digital parametric model of a vintage de Havilland DH.82 Tiger Moth biplane to support a restoration project. The restoration team has provided a geometry specification file located at /home/ga/Desktop/tiger_moth_spec.txt.

Read the specification file to obtain the key dimensions. Then, in OpenVSP, create a new multi-component model that captures the biplane's layout. You must add and configure:
1. A fuselage body (using a Fuselage or Stack component) matching the specified length.
2. An upper wing (WingGeom) with the specified span, chord, and dihedral.
3. A lower wing (WingGeom) with its specified span, chord, and dihedral.
4. Ensure the correct vertical separation (inter-plane gap) between the upper and lower wings by adjusting their Z-locations in the XForm tab.
5. Adjust the X-locations to set the forward stagger (upper wing ahead of lower wing).
6. Add a horizontal stabilizer (WingGeom) at the tail with the specified span.

All dimensions are in meters and degrees. Save the completed model to /home/ga/Documents/OpenVSP/tiger_moth_biplane.vsp3.
